sql_array: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO users (id, name, bio) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'Loves programming'), (2, 'Bob', NULL), (3, 'Charlie', 'Enjoys reading books...');
SELECT ucase(name) AS uppercase_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE ucase(name) <> name) AS different_case_count FROM users WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ucase(bio) AS uppercase_bio, COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE ucase(bio) <> '' GROUP BY ucase(bio) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT ucase(name) FROM (SELECT name, COUNT(*) AS name_count FROM users GROUP BY name HAVING name_count <> 1) AS subquery;
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE name <> ucase(name) OR bio IS NULL ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO users (id, name, bio) VALUES (4, 'Diana', 'A passionate artist'), (5, NULL, 'Playing with NULL');
SELECT ucase(name), ucase(bio) FROM users WHERE ucase(bio) IS NOT NULL AND ucase(name) <> 'ALICE' ORDER BY ucase(name) DESC;
SELECT DISTINCT ucase(name) FROM users WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users u2 WHERE ucase(u2.name) <> ucase(users.name) AND u2.id <> users.id);
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, product VARCHAR(100), FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO orders (order_id, user_id, product) VALUES (1, 1, 'Laptop'), (2, 2, NULL), (3, 3, 'Books'), (4, NULL, 'NULL product');
SELECT o.order_id, ucase(p.product) AS uppercase_product FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id WHERE o.product <> ucase('laptop');
SELECT ucase(name || ' - Order Summary') AS order_summary FROM users WHERE id <> (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE product IS NULL);
SELECT ucase(orders.product) FROM orders WHERE product IS NOT NULL AND user_id IS NOT NULL HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT ucase(group_concat(name)) AS all_users_names FROM users GROUP BY bio HAVING ucase(bio) <> '' ORDER BY ucase(name) ASC;
